name: Honda Civic (9th Gen)
engine: 1.8L SOHC i-VTEC I4 (R18Z1) on LX/EX — 2.4L DOHC i-VTEC I4 (K24Z7) in the Si — 1.5L + IMA in the Hybrid
reliability: 9/10 — one of the most trouble-free modern compacts. The headline item is the industry-wide Takata airbag-inflator recall this generation falls under, so verify it has been completed by VIN. The R18 drivetrain is exceptionally durable, and Honda's quick 2013 interior overhaul answered early cabin-quality criticism.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Low · Maintenance ~$350/yr · Class-leading economy and resale
popular_mods: A dedicated winter-tire set on a second set of wheels
All-weather floor liners and cargo protection
Updated head unit / phone integration on early cars
OEM-plus suspension refresh on higher-mileage examples
Honest take: the Si (2.4L K24) is the enthusiast version — the 1.8L is a keep-it-stock, runs-forever daily

generations.9th Gen (2012–2015): Sedan and coupe on the 140–143hp 1.8L R18; the Si moved to a torquier 2.4L K24Z7 (201–205hp) with a 6-speed manual. A natural-gas (CNG) variant and a 1.5L Hybrid were also offered.
generations.2013 interior overhaul: Honda heavily revised the dash materials and added equipment a year in, directly addressing criticism of the 2012 car — so 2013+ examples feel a clear notch nicer.

common_issues: Takata airbag-inflator recall (this generation is included) — verify completion by VIN
Early 2012 interior materials drew criticism (revised for 2013) — a perception issue, not a fault
i-MID infotainment quirks on some units
Front tire wear from alignment, typical of the platform
maintenance_tips: Confirm the Takata airbag recall is complete
Run 0W-20 full synthetic and follow the Maintenance Minder
Service the 5-speed automatic fluid with genuine Honda ATF around 30–60k miles
Check valve clearances around 100k (chain-driven, no timing belt)
